creative_brief: Split-screen visual: left side shows a supermarket styrofoam tray with generic beef and a long dotted-line map route across Australia. Right side shows a FarmThru brown paper package with a visible farm name label and a short direct arrow from farm to hub. Clean, warm lighting on the right. Muted, fluorescent feel on the left.
primary_text:
Last week I opened a pack of supermarket beef. The label said Australian. The fine print said it travelled 2,400km and sat in cold storage for up to three weeks.

Then I opened a box from FarmThru. Grass-fed beef from a named NSW farm. Days old, not weeks. No hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing.

Same country. Completely different food system.

FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. Zero wholesalers, zero middlemen. You order online, collect from the Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. No commitment. Order when you want.

We're building something worth owning. Be part of it.
headline: Same beef, two completely different stories
description: Farm-direct grocery. Days old, not weeks. Collect from Brookvale or get Sydney delivery.
